CFX.AreYouThereRequest.desc: Allows any CFX endpoint to determine if another particular CFX endpoint is present on a CFX network. The response sends basic information about the endpoint, including its CFX Handle, and network hostname / address.

fields.name: RequestNetworkUri
fields.desc: The network address / Uri to be used for requests to this endpoint

fields.name: RequestTargetAddress

CFX.EndpointConnected.desc: Sent when an Endpoint joins a CFX network after it has been established. Provides the same information as the response to the AreYouThereRequest message.

CFX.GetEndpointInformationResponse.desc: Allows any CFX endpoint to request the capabilities of a specified single endpoint. The response includes information about the endpoint, including its CFX Handle, and network hostname / address.

fields.desc: Dynamic structure that describes the details of this endpoint.

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: The result of the request

CFX.Heartbeat.desc: All endpoints are obligated to publish a Heartbeast message on all Publish type no less than once every 5 minutes. The class automatically performs this function for you when using the CFX SDK.

fields.name: ActiveFaults
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.7 ** Any faults currently active on this endpoint. Faults should be repeated here until cleared. Leaving this parameter as null means that active faults are not known. Setting it to an empty List implies positive knowledge that there are no active faults.

fields.name: ActiveRecipes
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.7 ** Any recipes currently active on this endpoint. Recipes may be specified either using their identifiers or by including recipe details such as expected cycle times depending on what information the endpoing is able to communicate. Leaving this parameter as null means that active recipes are not known. Setting it to an empty List implies positive knowledge that there are no active recipes. Multiple recipes may be active at once on a given endpoint.

fields.name: CFXHandle
fields.desc: The handle of the endpoint that is publishing the Heartbeat.

fields.name: HeartbeatFrequency
fields.desc: The amount of time to expect between Heartbeast messasges for this endpoint

CFX.NotSupportedResponse.desc: Allows a CFX endpoint to indicate to the sender of a request that it is not able to answer to this particular request There can be several reasons : - Unknown request/response message : appeared in a more recent version of CFX than the one used by the endp

fields.name: RequestResult
fields.desc: The result of the request

CFX.WhoIsThereRequest.desc: Allows any CFX endpoint to discover all of the other endpoints participating in this CFX network, and their capabilities. All other CFX endpoints matching the specified criteria must then respond to this broadcast.

fields.name: SupportedTopicQueryType
fields.desc: If the sender is interested only in certain types of endpoints, tbis property specifies how SupportedTopics list will be interpreted.

fields.name: SupportedTopics
fields.desc: An optional list of CFX topics. Only machines supporting any or all of these CFX topics will respond to this broadcast.

CFX.WhoIsThereResponse.desc: Allows any CFX endpoint to discover all of the other endpoints participating in this CFX network, and their capabilities. All other CFX endpoints must then respond to this broadcast, providing information about themselves.

fields.name: CFXHandle
fields.desc: The handle of the endpoint that is responding

fields.name: RequestNetworkUri
fields.desc: The network address / Uri to be used for requests to this endpoint

fields.name: RequestTargetAddress
fields.desc: The AMQP 1.0 target address to be used for requests to this endpoint

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: Result of the request.

CFX.InformationSystem.DataTransfer.FileT.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.4 ** Sent by an endpoint to another endpoint to initiate a file transfer. File transfers may proceed either from the initiator/requester to the recipient (PUSH Mode), OR from recipient to initiator/requester (PULL Mode).

fields.name: File
fields.desc: For file transfers initiated at the source (FileTransferDirection.Push), contains information about the file to be transferred. For file transfers initiated by the destination (FileTransferDirection.Pull), this property should be NULL. For FileTransferMode.InBand, this structure will contain the file payload (data) itself. For FileTransferMode.OutOfBand, this structure will contain a secure link/URL that can be used by the destination to obtain the file payload data.

fields.name: FileLocation
fields.desc: An optional property that may be used to indicate the location to the file on the source system. This need not be a physical path. It could be as virtual path, as in the case of a web or FTP server, or file share. Alternatively, it need not represent a traditional path at all. For example, it could be a lookup key to locate the file within a NoSQL database environment. The format of this property is dependent on the nature of the source system.

fields.name: FileTitle
fields.desc: The title of the file, EXCLUDING any path information, but INCLUDING file extension when appropriate.

fields.name: TransferDirection
fields.desc: Specifies the direction of transfer. File transfer may proceed from requestor to responder (Push), or from responder to requester (Pull). In Push mode, the file token is contained within the request itself. In Pull mode, the file token is contained within the response message.

fields.name: TransferMode
fields.desc: Specifies the mode of transfer. File data may be transferred within the CFX messages themselves (In-Band), or transferred via a separate secure protocol (Out-of-Band), such as HTTPS, SFTP, etc. Direct, in-band file transfer is not recommended for larger data files. Many AMQP message broker platforms limit the maximum total message size of an AMQP message. Microsoft's Azure message bus, for example, limits messages to 256KB for a standard subscription, and 1MB for a premium subscription. RabbitMQ recommends limiting messages to 128MB or less. Use OutOfBand transfer for larger file sizes.

CFX.InformationSystem.UnitValidation.Val.desc: Sent from a process endpoint in order to validate the identifier of the next production unit. Process endpoints, where configured, should send this request before allowing the next unit to enter the process.

fields.name: HermesIdentifier
fields.desc: The Hermes BoardId of the carrier, a PCB panel or single production unit. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc. HermesIdentifier will be transfered between all machines which support Hermes. The PrimaryIdentifier is containing a barcode information. Both can be transferred. Remarks Espcially when the line does not support the Hermes standard in the hole line, the Hermes Identifier can be unique only in the a part of the line. The Primary Identifier can be used to correlate the parts of hermes sublines to correlate this data as well.

fields.name: Lane
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.2 ** Lane identifier. Null if no specific lane

fields.name: PrimaryIdentifier
fields.desc: Identification used for the carrier (or the unit itself if no carrier)

fields.name: Units
fields.desc: List of structures that identify each specific instance of production unit that arrived (could be within a carrier or panel).

fields.name: Validations
fields.desc: List of validations to be performed”. Options are: UnitRouteValidation", "UnitStatusValidation"

CFX.InformationSystem.UnitValidation.Val.desc: Sent from a process endpoint in order to validate the identifier of the next production unit. Process endpoints, where configured, should send this request before allowing the next unit to enter the process.

The use of this property should be reserved to an endpoint that is able to know the location of all the MaterialPackage of the factory, like an ERP for example

fields.name: Carriers
fields.desc: There are two usage cases are available for this message: 1) Load MaterialCarrier directly to Endpoint (LocationIdentifier = Endpoint Slot Identifier, CarrierInformation = Carrier to be loaded) 2) Load MateriialCarrier to another MaterialCarrier (LocationIdentifier = Identifier of new parent carrier, CarrierInformation = Identifier of carrier to be loaded)

CFX.Materials.Storage.MaterialCarriersUn.desc: Sent when a material carrier (typcially containing 1 or more material packages) is unloaded at an endpoint.

fields.name: CarrierIdentifiers
fields.desc: List of carriers to be unloaded. NOTE - This message DOES NOT mean that the MaterialPackage is unloaded from the MaterialCarrier. Only that the MaterialCarrier is unloaded from its parent (slot on and Endpoint, or another MaterialCarrier).

CFX.Materials.Storage.MaterialsEmpty.desc: Sent when one or more material packages stored at a particular location become fully exhausted / depleted.

fields.name: EmptyMaterials
fields.desc: A list of the material locations where the depleted material packages are stored or loaded at the endpoint. Also includes the material package that is loaded at the location (if known). For example, if the endpoint is an SMT placement machine, the location identifies the feeder slot/position where the depleted material package is loaded.

CFX.Materials.Storage.MaterialsLoaded.desc: Sent when a material package (potentially contained within a material carrier) is loaded at an endpoint.

fields.name: Materials
fields.desc: There are three usage cases are available for this message: 1) Load MaterialPackage directly to Endpoint (LocationIdentifier = Endpoint Slot Identifier, CarrierInformation = null) 2) Load MaterialPackage to MaterialCarrier (LocationIdentifier = null, CarrierInformation = Identifier of carrier to be loaded) 3) Load MaterialPackage to MaterialCarrier AND Load MaterialCarrier to Endpoint (LocationIdentifier = Endpoint Slot Identifier, CarrierInformation = Identifier of carrier to be loaded)

CFX.Production.GetAvailableRecipesReques.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.4 ** This message is used to request a process endpoint for the available recipes.

fields.name: MaxCount
fields.desc: Max. number of recipes that may be returned with the response.

fields.name: Path
fields.desc: Pathname with wildcard, e.g. “myRecipes/CFX/A-Team*”.

CFX.Production.GetAvailableRecipesRespon.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.4 ** Response to a request of getting the available recipes. The response includes a list of recipes (name, revision), but not their data.

fields.name: ActualCount
fields.desc: The actual number of recipes that match the request.

fields.name: RecipeCount
fields.desc: The number of recipes in the response list

fields.name: Recipes
fields.desc: An optional list of the identified RecipeLean (RecipeName and Revision) structures

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: The result of the request

CFX.Production.GetRecipeRequest.desc: This message is used to request a process endpoint for the details of a named recipe. The response includes details of the recipe, depending on the classification of the process.

fields.name: RecipeName
fields.desc: The name of the recipe to be retrieved (may include full path, if applicable)

fields.name: Revision
fields.desc: Version number, e.g. “2.0” (Optional)

CFX.Production.GetRecipeResponse.desc: This message is used to request a process endpoint for the details of a named recipe. The response includes details of the recipe, depending on the classification of the process.

fields.name: Recipe
fields.desc: The recipe. This is a dynamic structure tha supports specialized recipe types for different types of equipment.

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: The result of the request

CFX.Production.GetRequiredSetupRequest.desc: Sent to a process endpoint to request the setup requirements of the active recipe. The response lists the required materials and tools, along with the locations where the materials/tools must be loaded.

fields.name: Lane
fields.desc: An optional property designating the specific Lane.

fields.name: Stage
fields.desc: An optional property designating the specific Stage.

CFX.Production.GetRequiredSetupResponse.desc: Response from a process endpoint to a request to obtain the setup requirements of the active recipe. The response lists the required materials and tools, along with the locations where the materials/tools must be loaded.

fields.name: Lane
fields.desc: An optional property designating the specific Lane associated with this setup.

fields.name: RecipeName
fields.desc: The name of the recipe associated with the required setup.

fields.name: RecipeRevision
fields.desc: The revision of the recipe that is required (eg. "A", "B", etc.)

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: Result of the request

fields.name: SetupRequirements
fields.desc: A structure indicating the setup requirements.

fields.name: Stage
fields.desc: An optional property designating the specific Stage associated with this setup.

fields.name: HermesIdentifier
fields.desc: The Hermes BoardId (GUID) of the carrier, a PCB panel or single production unit – optional, may be used when PrimaryIdentifier is not available.

fields.name: PrimaryIdentifier
fields.desc: The barcode, RFID, etc. that was most recently acquired by the scanner / reader. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc.

fields.name: UnitIdentifier
fields.desc: Unique ID of Production Unit, e.g. Barcode, to be used in case the Primary Identifier is not available and, thus, PrimaryIdentifier is left empty.

CFX.Production.LockStationRequest.desc: Request that the endpoint cease active operation (locked) as soon as practically possible at a process endpoint. A specific production lane or stage may be optionally specified. Includes a reason, and applies to all operations.

fields.name: Lane
fields.desc: An optional production lane. When specified, only that production lane shall be locked.

fields.name: Reason
fields.desc: Reason for the request to lock the station.

fields.name: Requestor
fields.desc: Identifies the originator of the request. (optional)

fields.name: Stage
fields.desc: An optional stage. When specified, only that stage shall be locked.

CFX.Production.LockStationResponse.desc: Response to a request that the endpoint cease active operation (locked) as soon as practically possible at a process endpoint.

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: The result of the request

CFX.Production.OperatorActivated.desc: Indicates that an operator is now active at or responsible for a process endpoint. Having multiple operators (each needs to be activated and deactivated separately) or an absence of an operator is possible.

fields.name: Operator
fields.desc: A structure which defines the Operator.

CFX.Production.OperatorDeactivated.desc: Indicates that an activated operator is no longer active or responsible at a process endpoint

fields.name: Operator
fields.desc: A structure which defines the Operator (optional)

CFX.Production.ReadingsRecorded.desc: A process endpoint uses this message to send a data object that has been acquired for example from a sensor or a reading taken during processing of the unit. This data is typically used as a traceability record.

fields.name: Readings
fields.desc: Dynamic structure providing a list of readings to be recorded.

fields.name: TransactionId
fields.desc: If the readings related to this message are associated with a work transaction, this property contains the Id of the transaction.

CFX.Production.RecipeActivated.desc: Sent by a process endpoint to indicate the activation of a recipe by its name

fields.name: ExpectedCycleTime
fields.desc: The total amount of time (in ms) that is expected to process one unit or group of units (as in the case of a carrier or panelized PCB), assuming no blocked or starved conditions at the station. This includes both productive and non-productive time, such as transfer, positioning, etc.

fields.name: ExpectedUnitsPerWorkTransaction
fields.desc: The number of units that are to be processed simulataneously by this recipe. For example, in the case of a 2 x 2 panelized PCB, this property would be 4 because 4 units (PCBs) are procesed at one time per work transaction. In the case that a station processes a variable number of units per transaction, this should represent the average number of units expected to be processed per transaction.

fields.name: ExpectedWorkTime
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.6 ** The total amount of productive time (in ms) that is expected to process one unit or group of units (as in the case of a carrier or panelized PCB), assuming no blocked or starved conditions at the station. This does not include any non-productive time, such as transfer, positioning, etc.

fields.name: NumberOfComponentsPerUnit

fields.name: RelevantSurface

fields.name: Revision

fields.name: Stage
fields.desc: An optional stage

fields.name: TargetQuantity
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.3 ** Identifies the target number of units to be produced by the newly activated recipe. This property is optional, but should be specified if known by the endpoint producing this message.

fields.name: WorkOrderIdentifier
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.3 ** Identifies the Work Order (or Batch) that will be executed by the newly activated recipe.

CFX.Production.RecipeModified.desc: Sent by a process endpoint to indicate that a change has been made to a specified named recipe.

fields.name: ModifiedBy
fields.desc: The operator who performed the modification. (optional)

fields.name: Notes
fields.desc: Any additional notes provided by the user who performed the modification.

fields.name: Reason
fields.desc: The reason for the modification.

fields.desc: The name of the recipe (may include full path, if applicable)

fields.name: Revision
fields.desc: Version number, e.g. “2.0” (Optional)

fields.name: WorkOrderIdentifier
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.6 ** Identifies the Work Order (or Batch) that is related to the modified recipe.

CFX.Production.SetupRequirementsChanged.desc: Sent whenever the setup requirement of materials, tools, etc. are changed for any reason at a process endpoint. This message contains a detailed listing of the required items, and their designated positions.

fields.name: Lane
fields.desc: An optional property designating the affected Lane.

fields.name: Stage
fields.desc: An optional property designating the affected Stage.

fields.name: CleaningSteps
fields.desc: A list of cleaning steps

fields.name: Tools
fields.desc: A list of the tools that were cleaned. It may contain SMTSqueegee or SMTStencil

CFX.Production.ToolsUsed.desc: Sent by a process endpoint when one or more tools are used in the course of performing an assembly operation.

CFX.Production.UnblockMaterialLocationsR.desc: Sent to a process endpoint to release a material locations block which was put into place by a previously sent BlockMaterialLocationsRequest

fields.name: Locations
fields.desc: A list of locations to be unblocked

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: The result of the request

CFX.Production.UnitsArrived.desc: Sent when production units physically arrives at a process endpoint, prior to any work or other activity commencing.

fields.name: HermesIdentifier
fields.desc: The Hermes BoardId of the carrier, a PCB panel or single production unit. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc. HermesIdentifier will be transfered between all machines which support Hermes. The PrimaryIdentifier is containing a barcode information. Both can be transferred. Remarks Espcially when the line does not support the Hermes standard in the hole line, the Hermes Identifier can be unique only in the a part of the line. The Primary Identifier can be used to correlate the parts of hermes sublines to correlate this data as well.

However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc.

fields.name: Units
fields.desc: An optional list of structures that identify each specific instance of production unit that arrived (if known). Could be individual units, or within a carrier, panel, etc.

CFX.Production.UnlockStationRequest.desc: Request that a process endpoint resume production, following a previous lock. The response indicates that the lock has been removed.

fields.name: Lane
fields.desc: An optional production lane. When specified, only that production lane shall be unlocked.

fields.name: Requestor
fields.desc: Information about the operator who is requesting that the stage be unlocked. (optional)

fields.name: Stage
fields.desc: An optional stage designating the particular production stage to be unlocked

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: The result of the request

CFX.Production.UpdateRecipeRequest.desc: This message is used to send a named recipe to a process endpoint. The message includes details of the recipe, depending on the classification of the process. The response indicates whether the recipe has been received correctly or not.

fields.name: Overwrite
fields.desc: If set to true, any existing Recipe at the Endpoint with the same name will be replaced by the Recipe provided in this request. If set to False, the update request will not succeed if a Recipe with the same name exists at the Endpoint.

fields.name: Reason
fields.desc: The reason for the update

fields.name: Recipe
fields.desc: The new Recipe to be sent to the Endpoint.

CFX.Production.UpdateRecipeResponse.desc: This message is used to send a named recipe to a process endpoint. The message includes details of the recipe, depending on the classification of the process. The response indicates whether the recipe has been received correctly or not.

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: The result of the request

fields.desc: The barcode, RFID, etc. that was most recently acquired by the scanner / reader. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc.

fields.name: RecipeName
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.1 ** The name of the recipe (may include full path, if applicable).

fields.name: RelevantSurface
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.1 ** For two-dimensional products, such as printed circuit assemblies, specifies the relevant surface that will be processed by the newly activated recipe.

fields.name: Revision
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.1 ** Optional: Version number, e.g. “2.0”.

fields.name: TransactionID
fields.desc: Transaction ID used to attach events and data during subsequent processing, until WorkCompleted

fields.name: UnitCount
fields.desc: The number of individual production units associated with this transaction. May be more than 1 in the case of a carrier or panel of multiple PCB’s.

fields.name: Units
fields.desc: Data that identifies each specific instance of production unit with a carrier or panel.

fields.name: WorkOrderIdentifier
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.1 ** Identifies the Work Order (or Batch) that will be executed by the newly activated recipe.

CFX.Production.Application.MaterialsAppl.desc: Sent when material is applied to a production unit, such as glue, adhesives, coatings, solder, paste, etc. ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.

fields.name: AppliedMaterials
fields.desc: A list of the specific materials which were applied.

fields.name: TransactionId
fields.desc: The id of the work transaction with which this message is associated.

CFX.Production.Application.MaterialsUnap.desc: Sent when material is unapplied or removed from a production unit, as in the case of paste being wiped clean, for example.

fields.name: TransactionId
fields.desc: The id of the work transaction with which this message is associated.

fields.name: UnappliedMaterials
fields.desc: A list of materials which have been uninstalled.

fields.name: HeaderData
fields.desc: General data values that apply across the selective process cycle

fields.name: SolderedPCBs
fields.desc: Details of PCB that has been processed.

fields.name: TransactionId
fields.desc: The id of the work transaction with which this inspection session is associated.

CFX.Production.Assembly.MaterialsInstall.desc: Sent by a process endpoint when one or more materials (see note) are installed on to a production unit. This message is typically sent at the completion of production on a unit or group of units at the endpoint, or, at the end of each stage.

fields.name: InstalledMaterials
fields.desc: A list of the specific materials which were installed.

fields.name: TransactionId
fields.desc: The id of the work transaction with which this installation is associated.

CFX.Production.Assembly.MaterialsUninsta.desc: Sent by a process endpoint when one or more materials (see note) are removed from a production unit. This message is typically sent at the completion of a production unit or group of units at the endpoint, or, at the end of each stage.

fields.name: TransactionId
fields.desc: The id of the work transaction with which this uninstallation is associated.

fields.name: UninstalledMaterials
fields.desc: A list of materials which have been uninstalled.

CFX.Production.Assembly.UnitsPersonalize.desc: Sent by a process endpoint when a configuration or assignment is made (example MAC Address)

fields.name: PersonalizedUnits
fields.desc: A list of production units that have been personalized, including the personalization characteristics of each.

fields.name: TransactionId
fields.desc: The id of the work transaction with which this installation is associated.

CFX.Production.Assembly.PressInsertion.C.desc: Sent by a press insertion machine when a condition has been completed

fields.name: ResultOfCondition
fields.desc: An enumeration indicating the result of the condition

fields.name: TransactionID
fields.desc: Related Transaction ID specified previously by WorkStarted Message

CFX.Production.Assembly.PressInsertion.G.desc: This message is used to request a process endpoint for permission to proceed with the recipe based on the results of a condition sequence action

fields.name: ConditionStep
fields.desc: Describes a individual step of a Condition sequence that must be validated

fields.name: Data
fields.desc: The data to validate

fields.name: TransactionID
fields.desc: Related Transaction ID specified previously by WorkStarted Message

CFX.Production.Assembly.PressInsertion.G.desc: This message is used to grant or reject permission for a process endpoint to proceed with the recipe based on the results of a condition sequence action.

fields.name: ConditionStep
fields.desc: Describes a individual step of a Condition sequence that must be validated

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: The result of the request

fields.name: TransactionID
fields.desc: Related Transaction ID specified previously by WorkStarted Message

fields.name: MagazineId
fields.desc: Barcode of a magazine, required to identify the magazine.

CFX.Production.Hermes.GetMagazineDataRes.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.3 ** Reponse to a request by a Hermes enabled endpoint to acquire information related to a particular magazine.

fields.name: MagazineData
fields.desc: Barcode of a magazine, required to identify the magazine.

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: Result of the request

CFX.Production.Hermes.GetWorkOrderDataRe.desc: Used by an endpoint to acquire information related to a particular work order (typically at the beginning of a Hermes enabled line). This information would typically then be passed down the line through the Hermes protocol / mechanism.

fields.name: UnitIdentifier
fields.desc: (OPTIONAL) If work order informatoin is not known, an endpoint may also obtain work order data by instead populating this property with the identifier of a production unit that is associated with a work order and known to an upper level control system. The upper level control system will then look up the associated work order, and return the appropriate work order information.

CFX.Production.Hermes.MagazineDeparted.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.3 ** Event triggered by a Hermes enabled endpoint when a magazine departed.

fields.desc: Barcode of a magazine, required to identify the magazine.

CFX.Production.LoadingAndUnloading.Units.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.2 ** Sent when a unit is unloaded into any form of carrier, including fixtures, pallets, trays, tubs, totes, carts, etc. Associates unit with a carrier.

fields.name: UnitCount
fields.desc: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.0 ** The number of individual production units

fields.desc: A list of the specific units that were unloaded along with positions they were unloaded to.

CFX.Production.Processing.UnitsProcessed.desc: Sent when an endpoint processes one or more production units within the scope of a work transaction. Contains dynamic structures that vary based upon the type of processing that was performed.

fields.name: CommonProcessData

fields.desc: A list of structures containing the processing data for each production units processed by this transaction.

CFX.Sensor.Identification.IdentifiersRea.desc: Sent by an identification device (barcode scanner, RFID reader, etc.

fields.name: HermesIdentifier
fields.desc: The Hermes BoardId of the carrier, a PCB panel or single production unit. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc. HermesIdentifier will be transfered between all machines which support Hermes. The PrimaryIdentifier is containing a barcode information. Both can be transferred. Remarks Espcially when the line does not support the Hermes standard in the hole line, the Hermes Identifier can be unique only in the a part of the line. The Primary Identifier can be used to correlate the parts of hermes sublines to correlate this data as well.

fields.name: PrimaryIdentifier
fields.desc: The barcode, RFID, etc. that was most recently acquired by the scanner / reader. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc.

fields.name: Units
fields.desc: An optional list of actual production unit identifiers, in the case that multiple production units are moving through the process, and the sensor is capable if reading multiple identifiers.

The PrimaryIdentifier is containing a barcode information. Both can be transferred. Remarks Espcially when the line does not support the Hermes standard in the hole line, the Hermes Identifier can be unique only in the a part of the line. The Primary Identifier can be used to correlate the parts of hermes sublines to correlate this data as well.

fields.desc: The barcode, RFID, etc. that was most recently acquired by the scanner / reader. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc.

fields.name: Result
fields.desc: The result of the request

fields.name: Units
fields.desc: An optional list of actual production unit identifiers, in the case that multiple production units are moving through the process, and the sensor is capable if reading multiple identifiers.
